01_initial_request.input_data: AlphaFold predicted CDK20 protein structure, text and OMICs data from 10 datasets for hepatocellular carcinoma
01_initial_request.user_intent: To rapidly identify de novo small molecule hits for a novel therapeutic target associated with hepatocellular carcinoma using AI-powered drug discovery and AlphaFold predicted structures
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identification of a confirmed hit molecule with measurable binding affinity (Kd) or nanomolar potency

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) has high prevalence and lack of effective treatments. [Gap] Need to identify novel therapeutic targets for HCC treatment. [Decision] To achieve target identification, chose to use PandaOmics to perform multi-omics analysis on 10 HCC datasets, expecting to obtain ranked list of target hypotheses.

parameters.datasets: 10 datasets for HCC (GSE36376, GSE107170, GSE102079, GSE45267, GSE133039, GSE104766, GSE77314, GSE60502, E-MTAB-5905, TCGA-LIHC)
parameters.scoring_approach: combination of multiple scores derived from text and OMICs data

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated ranked list of target hypotheses for HCC

references.relevance: Describes PandaOmics platform for target identification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PandaOmics generated ranked list of target hypotheses. [Gap] Need to filter targets based on first-in-class scenario criteria. [Decision] To achieve target prioritization, chose to use PandaOmics to perform multidimensional filtration, expecting to obtain top 20 targets.

parameters.filtering_criteria: novelty, accessibility by biologics, safety, small molecule accessibility, tissue specificity, crystal structure availability
parameters.first_in_class_scenario: protein druggable by small molecules, target novel, not in phase 1 clinical trials in last 3 years, not target of previously approved drugs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained list of top 20 targets satisfying first-in-class scenario

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Top 20 targets identified from PandaOmics filtering. [Gap] Need to select final target for drug discovery. [Decision] To achieve target selection, chose to use manual curation to evaluate candidates, expecting to obtain CDK20 as final target.

parameters.selection_criteria: highest scores aligned with first-in-class scenario, strong disease association, limited experimental structure information, shortage of approved drugs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: CDK20 selected as initial target for HCC treatment

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CDK20 selected as target but lacks experimental structure. [Gap] Need 3D structure for structure-based drug design. [Decision] To achieve structure acquisition, chose to use AlphaFold DB to retrieve predicted structure, expecting to obtain CDK20 protein structure model.

parameters.confidence_level: high confidence level overall except for C-terminal

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AlphaFold structure retrieved but C-terminal blocks ATP pocket. [Gap] C-terminal conformation not favorable for inhibitor design. [Decision] To achieve usable structure for molecule generation, chose to use structure modification to remove C-terminal, expecting to obtain truncated structure Met1-Ile302.

parameters.reason: C-terminal occupies ATP pocket with very low confidence

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained truncated CDK20 structure (Met1-Ile302) for molecule generation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Truncated CDK20 structure prepared. [Gap] Need to identify binding sites for compound generation. [Decision] To achieve binding site identification, chose to use Chemistry42 to perform energy-based pocket detection, expecting to obtain identified binding pockets.

parameters.pocket_scoring: pocket volume, surface, and depth descriptors
parameters.identified_pocket: shallow ATP binding pocket with estimated volume of around 150 Å³

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified ATP binding pocket with DFG-in conformation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ATP binding pocket identified. [Gap] Need to generate novel molecules targeting this pocket. [Decision] To achieve molecule generation, chose to use Chemistry42 to perform pocket-based compound generation, expecting to obtain novel molecule structures.

parameters.structural_guidance: 3D shape of pocket, pocket volume, spatial arrangements of atoms

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 8918 molecules generated. [Gap] Need to prioritize molecules for synthesis. [Decision] To achieve molecule prioritization, chose to use Chemistry42 to perform molecular docking, expecting to obtain docking scores for generated molecules.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Completed molecular docking for all generated molecules

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Molecular docking completed. [Gap] Need to select diverse compounds for synthesis. [Decision] To achieve compound selection, chose to use Chemistry42 to perform clustering, expecting to obtain prioritized molecules with diverse structures.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Prioritized 54 molecules, selected 7 compounds for synthesis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 7 compounds selected computationally. [Gap] Need to physically produce compounds for testing. [Decision] To achieve compound availability, chose to use chemical synthesis to produce selected compounds, expecting to obtain physical compounds for biological testing.

[Background] 7 compounds synthesized. [Gap] Need to measure binding affinity to CDK20. [Decision] To achieve binding affinity measurement, chose to use KINOMEscan service to perform kinase binding assay, expecting to obtain Kd values for all compounds.

parameters.assay_type: CDK20 human CMGC kinase binding assay

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding assay completed for 7 compounds. [Gap] Need to identify hit molecule from first round. [Decision] To achieve hit identification, chose to use data analysis to evaluate binding results, expecting to obtain confirmed hit molecule with measurable Kd.

parameters.fitting_method: non-linear least squares fit with Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ISM042-2-001 demonstrated Kd value of 9.2±0.5 μM (n=3)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] First hit ISM042-2-001 identified with micromolar Kd. [Gap] Need to improve binding affinity. [Decision] To achieve potency improvement, chose to use Chemistry42 to perform second-round compound generation based on predicted binding pose, expecting to obtain more potent molecules.

parameters.generation_strategy_1: occupation of hydrophobic pocket near gatekeeper region with functional groups on quinazoline ring
parameters.generation_strategy_2: modifications of pyrrole-2-carboxamide group to access solvation region and interact with acidic residues Asp87 or Glu90
parameters.guidance: predicted binding pose and potency data from first round

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 16 novel molecules for second round

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 16 molecules generated in second round. [Gap] Need to physically produce compounds for testing. [Decision] To achieve compound availability, chose to use chemical synthesis to produce selected compounds, expecting to obtain physical compounds for biological testing.

[Background] 6 compounds synthesized in second round. [Gap] Need to measure binding affinity to CDK20. [Decision] To achieve binding affinity measurement, chose to use KINOMEscan service to perform kinase binding assay, expecting to obtain Kd values for second round compounds.

[Background] Second round binding assay completed. [Gap] Need to identify improved hit molecule. [Decision] To achieve hit identification, chose to use data analysis to evaluate binding results, expecting to obtain more potent hit molecule.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ISM042-2-048 demonstrated Kd value of 566.7±256.2 nM (n=3)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ISM042-2-048 identified with nanomolar Kd. [Gap] Need to confirm kinase inhibitory activity. [Decision] To achieve activity confirmation, chose to use Reaction Biology Corp to perform kinase activity assay, expecting to obtain IC50 value for CDK20 inhibition.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ISM042-2-048 showed IC50 value of 33.4±22.6 nM (n=3) in CDK20 kinase activity assay

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Kinase activity confirmed with nanomolar IC50. [Gap] Need to evaluate selective anti-proliferation activity in HCC cells. [Decision] To achieve cellular activity evaluation, chose to use Celltiter Glo assay to perform cell viability assay, expecting to obtain IC50 values in HCC and counter screen cell lines.

parameters.cell_lines: Huh7 (HCC with CDK20 overexpression) and HEK293 (counter screen)
parameters.cell_seeding: Huh7: 650 cells per well; HEK293: 300 cells per well

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ISM042-2-048 showed IC50 of 208.7±3.3 nM in Huh7 and 1706.7±670.0 nM in HEK293

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multi-level validation including kinase binding assay (KINOMEscan), kinase activity assay (33PanQinase), and cell viability assay (Celltiter Glo)

binding_affinity_Kd.interpretation: Confirmed hit molecule ISM042-2-048 with nanomolar binding affinity to CDK20, representing 15-fold improvement over first round hit

kinase_activity_IC50.interpretation: Demonstrated potent CDK20 inhibitory activity in kinase activity assay

cell_viability_Huh7_IC50.interpretation: Showed selective anti-proliferation activity in HCC cell line with CDK20 overexpression

cell_viability_HEK293_IC50.interpretation: Counter screen cell line showed 8-fold less sensitivity, confirming selectivity

timeline.interpretation: First hit identified within 30 days from target selection

compounds_synthesized.interpretation: Only 13 compounds synthesized total (7 in first round, 6 in second round) to achieve confirmed hit

03_success_verification.final_verdict: SUCCESS - The research objective was fully achieved. A confirmed hit molecule (ISM042-2-048) was identified with measurable nanomolar binding affinity (Kd = 566.7±256.2 nM) and nanomolar potency (IC50 = 33.4±22.6 nM) for CDK20, a novel therapeutic target for hepatocellular carcinoma. This represents the first demonstration of applying AlphaFold predicted structures to successfully identify a confirmed hit in drug discovery.
